this is incredibly noisy and pointless

The culprit is the `pc awareness --hook PostToolUse` hook at line 28-39 — it dumps the full peer roster on every tool call. Tenex-edge intentionally doesn't wire PostToolUse for Claude Code (per the wiki), so this is just legacy noise from the old `pc` system. Removing it.
[Request interrupted by user for tool use]
no, fucking fix the hook to produce something useful! not just keep it broken and disabled!
Let me check what tenex-edge supports for post-tool-use and what turn-check produces.
it should provide an awareness of what the other sessions are doing in the project with actual interesting stuff with the delta of what sessions titles gated by deltas
Now I have everything. Let me implement. First, the schema migration + column in `turn_state`:
before changing code explain to me what agents will see per your design. Let's agree on the goal.

Done. Here's where things stand.

## What I built
Replaced the noisy `pc awareness` PostToolUse firehose with **delta-gated, project-scoped sibling awareness** in tenex-edge. On a PostToolUse, an agent now sees — *only when something changed in this project*, at most once per 60s, never about itself:

```
tenex-edge fabric — changes since your last check:
  ↻ developer@tenex-edge [session 0afc3cf4] — Improve tmux prompts — editing hooks.rs · working
  ↻ sib@tenex-edge [session 7f1881] — Refactor tmux · idle
```

Title **and** activity (you asked for it), idle transitions shown (`· idle`), 60s debounce. Otherwise: **no output**. Direct inbox messages still surface immediately, unchanged.

## Mechanics
- `turn_state.last_check_at` cursor, reset each turn; `turn_check_due()` gates to ≥60s and fail-silent when not mid-turn. The cursor write is safe — turn_check is daemon-mediated, single writer.
- `list_status_changes_since` now returns the activity line too.
- Claude Code PostToolUse only reads context from `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` (plain stdout is ignored — *this is why it was deferred*). New `EmitFormat` selects that envelope only for claude-code PostToolUse; UserPromptSubmit/opencode stay plain, Codex stays `systemMessage`.

## Verification
- **189 unit tests pass**, including new tests for the 60s gate, cursor advance, self-exclusion, activity rendering, and idle transitions. Zero new clippy warnings.
- I could **not** run a live end-to-end smoke test — every CLI call to the daemon got SIGKILLed (137) in this environment (version-skew with the old daemon + a concurrent `cargo test` worktree run hammering resources). The logic is covered by tests, but treat live behavior as unconfirmed until the daemon cycles.

## Commits (on master)
- `0f5dad27` — my hook code (6 `.rs` files only)
- `bc9b2e10` — preserved the peer docs/wiki work (excluded `_citations.log`)

Peer commit `02e05a7c` (drop-presence) landed on master mid-task; my build/tests ran against it, so we're consistent. Not pushed (you didn't ask).

## ⚠️ One thing before this works live
The **running daemon (pid 7990) is from yesterday 18:32** — it predates the new binary (built 12:06 today). It will serve the *old* `turn_check` (inbox-peek only, no delta) until it restarts on the new binary. The hook is wired and the new CLI is installed, but **the delta half won't fire until the daemon cycles**.

those session ids look nothing like the session ids I see in tenex-edge tmux or who -- I'm going to guess thats the wrong format that we use to send messages to -- so that format needs to be changed to the proper format, not the raw UUID

Done — committed as `c9a6ea06`.

The delta now renders the **6-char `session_short_code`** for both the status-change line (`↻ … [session a1b2c3] …`) and the joined-peer line — identical to what `who` and tmux show, so it's copy-pasteable into `send --to`. The joined-peer line was doubly wrong before: it used `pubkey_short()` (the formatter explicitly documented as invalid for session ids) on a session id. Locked with a test asserting the short code is present and the raw id never leaks. 189 tests pass.

**Deployment:** binary installed (12:48). The live deltas you're still seeing with raw UUIDs come from the daemon running my *first* commit — it auto-cycled to that one (which is why deltas started appearing at all), so it should pick this up the same way on its next version-skew restart.
